metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Corrected per official rulebook, BGG FAQ, UltraBoardGames rules reference.

key_rulings.text: On your turn, place your Action token on one section of your Player Mat. You cannot repeat the same action you took on your previous turn.
A Seeker without resources may move up to 2 spaces. A Seeker carrying resources may move only 1 space.
If the attacker and defender have equal totals, the attacker wins.
If your Friendship is below 3, you cannot earn any trophies until you restore it to 3 or higher.
Once a trophy is earned, it cannot be lost, even if the condition that earned it is no longer met.
You may earn at most 1 trophy per turn, except during the Grand Finale.
To earn the Apple Delivery trophy, deliver exactly 4 apples to Castle Everfree in a single turn. Same for gems.
When you Seek, placing a resource on a space occupied by an opponent's Seeker grants you +1 Friendship per resource placed there.
Bake Pies: remove 2 apples, gain 2 pies. Conjure Spell: remove 2 gems, draw 1 spell. Power Up: remove 1 apple + 1 gem, draft a power-up tile.
The Pie Fight Victory trophy can be earned by winning as attacker OR defender. It is the only trophy that can be earned on another player's turn.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Tiles
- **1 game board** (double-sided) — A hex-grid map of the Kingdom of Pomme. Castle Everfree sits at the center. Territories surround it, with base camp spaces around the outer edge. Pink-bordered spaces are **portals** — they connect across the board.
- **7 base camp tiles** — One for each animal kingdom. Mark where your seekers start and retreat to after losing a pie fight.
- **16 power-up tiles** (8 Move, 8 Make) — Double-sided tiles drafted during the Make action. Each permanently upgrades one of your actions.

#### Miniatures & Tokens
- **14 seeker miniatures** — 2 per animal kingdom (7 kingdoms in the base game). Each pair shares a color.
- **28 trophy tokens** (4 per player) — Place these on the Trophy Track when you earn trophies.
- **7 friendship tokens** — One per player, tracked on the Friendship Track (range 0-8, starts at 3).
- **7 pie tokens** — One per player, tracked on the Pie Track (range 0-10, starts at 3).
- **7 action tokens** — Placed on your Player Mat to show which action you chose.

#### Resources
- **24 apple tokens** — Red acrylic tokens. One of the two resource types.
- **25 gem tokens** — Blue acrylic tokens. The other resource type.
- **12 quest tokens** — Placed on the board via Seek. Landing on one triggers a quest.

#### Cards & Dice
- **12 quest cards** — Each presents two options with different friendship consequences.
- **30 magic spell cards** — One-time-use abilities. Can also be attached to pie fights for extra strength.
- **8 personality cards** — Secret cards dealt at setup. Each modifies one trophy requirement, making it easier to earn.
- **5 custom dice** — Red dice generate apples, blue dice generate gems, gold dice generate quest tokens. Each face shows a region on the board.
- **6 player mats** — Track your action choices and power-up slots.
- **2 pie fight dials** — Secret dials for setting your pie bid during combat.

#### Solo Mode
- **1 Automountie board + tokens** — AI opponent for solo play.

#### Other
- **1 setup tile** — Determines starting resource placement.
- **1 painting guide + achievement sheet**
- **2 rulebooks** (multiplayer + solo)

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
My Little Scythe is a race to earn **4 trophies**. There are 8 different trophy categories, and you only need 4 of them. On each turn, you take one of three actions — Move, Seek, or Make — but you **cannot repeat the same action** you took last turn. Explore the Kingdom of Pomme, collect resources, deliver them to Castle Everfree, complete quests, fight pie fights, and maintain your friendship.

#### Setting Up the Board
1. **Unfold the game board** in the center of the table.
2. **Each player chooses an animal kingdom.** Take the 2 matching seeker miniatures, 1 base camp tile, 1 action token, 4 trophy tokens, and 1 player mat.
3. **Place your base camp tile** on the board per the player count positions shown.
4. **Place both seekers** on your base camp.
5. **Place your friendship token** on **level 3** of the Friendship Track.
6. **Place your pie token** on **level 3** of the Pie Track.
7. **Shuffle quest cards** and place face-down near the board.
8. **Shuffle spell cards** and deal **1 spell** to each player (keep secret). Place the rest face-down.
9. **Separate power-up tiles** into Move and Make piles, shuffle each face-down.
10. **Place the setup tile** on Castle Everfree; flip it to populate starting resources on the board.
11. **Deal 1 personality card** to each player (keep secret until you choose to reveal it).
12. **Determine first player** randomly. Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Checkpoint
Each player should have: 2 seekers on their base camp, friendship at 3, pies at 3, 1 spell card in hand, 1 secret personality card, 4 trophy tokens, a player mat, and an action token. Resources from the setup tile should be placed on the board.

subtopics.title: Turn Flow
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
Place your action token on **one** section of your player mat: **Move**, **Seek**, or **Make**. You **cannot choose the same action** you took on your previous turn. Resolve the action, then check for trophies (max 1 trophy per turn). Play passes clockwise.

#### Action 1: Move
Move **both** of your seekers (each independently):
- **Without resources:** A seeker can move up to **2 spaces**.
- **Carrying resources (apples/gems):** A seeker can move only **1 space**, carrying any resources from that space.

**Portals:** Pink-bordered spaces are portals — they are adjacent to each other. Moving between portals costs 1 movement.

**Picking up resources:** When a seeker enters a space with resources, it picks them up and carries them. Carried resources limit that seeker to 1-space movement.

**Delivering to Castle Everfree:** When a seeker carrying resources enters Castle Everfree, all carried resources are deposited. Delivering **4 apples in a single turn** earns the Apple Delivery trophy. Delivering **4 gems in a single turn** earns the Gem Delivery trophy. Both seekers can contribute to the total. After delivery, seekers return to your base camp and you are **rejuvenated** (gain 1 spell OR 2 pies; action token is removed from mat).

**Entering an occupied space:** If your seeker moves onto a space with an opponent's seeker, a **pie fight** is triggered (see below).

After all movement, resolve any **quests** on your seekers' spaces.

#### Action 2: Seek
Roll the **dice** shown on your player mat's Seek section. Each die shows a **region** on the board:
- **Red dice** → Place 1 apple token from the supply on any space in the indicated region.
- **Blue dice** → Place 1 gem token on any space in the indicated region.
- **Gold dice** → Place 1 quest token on any **unoccupied** space in the indicated region.

You choose the exact space within each region. **Friendship bonus:** Placing a resource on a space occupied by an opponent's seeker grants you **+1 friendship** per resource placed there.

#### Action 3: Make (Choose One)
Spend resources from your seekers' spaces:
- **Bake Pies** — Remove **2 apples** → gain **2 pies** on the Pie Track (max 10).
- **Conjure Spell** — Remove **2 gems** → draw **1 magic spell card** (no hand limit).
- **Power Up** — Remove **1 apple + 1 gem** → draw the top 3 tiles from either the Move or Make power-up pile. Keep 1, return 2 to the bottom. Place the chosen tile on your player mat, permanently upgrading that action.

subtopics.title: Pie Fights, Quests & Friendship
subtopics.content:
#### Pie Fights (Combat)
A pie fight triggers when your seeker moves onto a space occupied by an opponent's seeker.

1. **The attacker loses 1 friendship immediately.**
2. Both players **secretly set** their pie fight dial to a number of pies (0 to current pie total). For each seeker involved, a player may attach **1 magic spell card** to add its value (2-5) to their total.
3. Both players **reveal simultaneously**.
4. **Highest total wins. Ties go to the ATTACKER.**
5. All bid pies are **spent** (reduce Pie Track). Used spells are discarded.
6. The **loser's seekers** retreat to their base camp, dropping all carried resources on the battle space.
7. The **winner** earns the **Pie Fight Victory trophy** (if not already earned).
8. The **loser** is **rejuvenated** — gain 1 spell OR 2 pies, and their action token is removed from their mat (freeing up action choice next turn).

**Key details:**
- You CAN fight with 0 pies.
- Pie Fight Victory is the **only trophy** earnable on another player's turn (when defending).
- Spell cards add 2-5 to your total, making them powerful combat insurance.

#### Quests
Quest tokens appear on the board via the Seek action (gold dice). After completing your Move action (including any pie fights), if either seeker occupies a space with a quest token:
1. Remove the quest token from the board.
2. Draw 1 quest card.
3. Choose: **Option 1** (usually generous — smaller reward, +friendship), **Option 2** (usually greedy — bigger reward, -friendship), or **"No Thanks"** (return card to bottom of deck — does NOT count toward Quest trophy).

Completing **2 quests** (choosing Option 1 or 2) earns the **Quest trophy**.

#### The Friendship Track
Friendship ranges from **0 to 8**. Everyone starts at **3**.

**Friendship goes up when you:**
- Place resources on an opponent's seeker's space during Seek (+1 per resource)
- Choose generous quest options
- Use certain spell effects

**Friendship goes down when you:**
- Initiate a pie fight (-1)
- Choose greedy quest options

**CRITICAL RULE:** If your friendship drops **below 3**, you are **locked out of earning ANY trophies** until you restore it to 3 or higher. This is the game's primary anti-aggression mechanism.

Reaching friendship **level 8** earns the **Friendship trophy**.

subtopics.title: Trophies & Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### The 8 Trophy Categories
You need **4 trophies** to trigger the endgame. Each can be earned once per player. You may earn **at most 1 trophy per turn** (except during the Grand Finale).

1. **Friendship Trophy** — Reach level **8** on the Friendship Track.
2. **Pie Trophy** — Reach level **8** on the Pie Track.
3. **Power Ups Trophy** — Possess **both** a Move power-up tile AND a Make power-up tile simultaneously.
4. **Spell Trophy** — Have **3 or more** magic spell cards in hand at once.
5. **Apple Delivery Trophy** — Deliver exactly **4 apples** to Castle Everfree in a **single turn** (both seekers can contribute).
6. **Gem Delivery Trophy** — Deliver exactly **4 gems** to Castle Everfree in a **single turn** (both seekers can contribute).
7. **Quest Trophy** — Complete **2 quest cards** (choosing Option 1 or 2; "No Thanks" doesn't count).
8. **Pie Fight Victory Trophy** — **Win** a pie fight (as attacker OR defender). Only trophy earnable on another player's turn.

**Trophies are permanent.** If you earn the Pie trophy at level 8 and then spend pies, the trophy stays. Same for Friendship, Spells, etc.

**Friendship lock-out:** If your friendship is **below 3**, you cannot earn any trophies until restored.

#### Personality Cards
Each player has a secret personality card dealt during setup. It modifies one trophy's requirement, making it easier to earn. You may reveal it at any time — strategically time the reveal for maximum advantage.

#### Endgame (Grand Finale)
When any player places their **4th trophy**, the Grand Finale triggers. Finish the current round so all players have had equal turns. During the Grand Finale, the 1-trophy-per-turn limit is lifted.

#### Winning
1. The player with the **most trophies** wins.
2. Tied on trophies? **Higher friendship** wins.
3. Still tied? **More pies** remaining wins.
4. Still tied? **Shared victory**.

#### Solo Mode (Automountie)
The Automountie is an AI opponent controlled by action tokens and a decision flowchart. It earns trophies using the same rules. You must reach 4 trophies before it does.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Moves (Turns 1-3)
- **Turn 1: Seek.** Roll dice and place resources near your seekers. Place some on opponents' seeker spaces to gain +1 friendship per resource — a free friendship boost while setting up the board.
- **Turn 2: Move.** Send your seekers to collect resources you placed. Remember: carrying resources limits movement to 1 space, so place resources close.
- **Turn 3: Make.** Spend resources to Power Up (1 apple + 1 gem). Draft a power-up tile. Getting your first power-up early pays dividends all game.

#### The Fastest Path to 4 Trophies
The most reliable strategy hits these 4:
1. **Power Ups** — Get both a Move AND Make power-up (costs 2 apples + 2 gems total across 2 Make actions). This is the most reliable trophy.
2. **Friendship** — Reach level 8. Start at 3, gain +1 each time you place resources on opponents during Seek, plus generous quest choices. Avoid pie fights early.
3. **Spells** — Hold 3 spell cards simultaneously. Each Conjure Spell action (2 gems) draws 1 card. Three Make actions gets you there.
4. **Quest** — Complete 2 quests. Move seekers to quest token spaces and choose Option 1 or 2.

#### Key Rule: Don't Drop Below Friendship 3
This is the most important rule for beginners. If your friendship drops below 3, you are **completely locked out of earning trophies**. Every pie fight costs 1 friendship. Starting at 3, a single unprepared fight drops you to 2 — trophy lock-out. Build friendship above 3 before considering combat.

#### When to Fight
Combat earns a trophy but costs 1 friendship. The ideal time:
- **Friendship 5 or higher** — you can afford the -1.
- **You have spell cards** to boost your dial (each adds 2-5 to your total).
- **Your opponent is carrying resources** — if you win, they drop everything.
- **Ties go to the attacker** — so even a close fight favors you if you're attacking.

#### Seek Friendship Strategy
Placing resources on opponents' spaces during Seek gives +1 friendship per resource. This is **free friendship** — you're seeding the board AND gaining friendship simultaneously. Aim to gain 2-3 friendship per Seek action by targeting occupied spaces.

#### Action Restriction
You cannot repeat the same action twice in a row. Plan your turns in pairs: Seek → Move (collect what you placed), Move → Make (spend what you collected), Make → Seek (refill the board).

#### Common Mistakes
- **Fighting at friendship 3.** One fight drops you to 2 and locks you out of trophies. Build friendship first.
- **Forgetting the action restriction.** You can't Seek twice in a row. Plan alternating actions.
- **Ignoring spells in combat.** Spell cards add 2-5 to your pie fight total. Hoarding 3 spells gets you the Spell trophy AND makes you a combat threat.
- **Delivering fewer than 4.** You need exactly 4 apples (or 4 gems) delivered in a single turn for the trophy. Delivering 3 earns nothing. Use both seekers to hit the 4-resource threshold.
- **Revealing your personality card too early.** Wait until the optimal moment — the surprise factor is part of its value.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of My Little Scythe

**After Setup**: Each player has 2 seekers at base camp, friendship at 3, pies at 3, 1 spell card, 1 secret personality card, and 4 trophy tokens. Resources from the setup tile are scattered on the board.

**Turn 1 — Seek**: Roll your dice. Say you roll 2 red (apples) and 2 blue (gems). Place apples and gems on the board — put some near your seekers for easy pickup. Place at least 1 resource on an opponent's seeker space to gain +1 friendship (now at 4).

**Turn 2 — Move** (can't repeat Seek): Move both seekers toward the resources you placed. Without resources, each seeker moves up to 2 spaces. Pick up apples and gems as you enter their spaces. Once carrying resources, movement drops to 1 space.

**Turn 3 — Make** (can't repeat Move): Your seekers are carrying resources. Spend 1 apple + 1 gem to Power Up — draw 3 tiles from the Move pile, keep 1, return 2. Place the power-up on your player mat. One more power-up and you earn the **Power Ups trophy**.

**Turn 4 — Seek** (can't repeat Make): Roll dice again. Place resources near your seekers and on opponents' spaces for friendship. If a gold die appears, place a quest token on the board.

**Turn 5 — Move**: Collect resources. If there's a quest token on your path, land on it — draw a quest card and choose an option. That's quest 1 of 2 toward the **Quest trophy**.

**Turn 6 — Make**: Spend 1 apple + 1 gem for a second Power Up (this time from the Make pile). You now have both Move and Make power-ups → earn the **Power Ups trophy**! That's 1 of 4.

**Mid-Game**: Continue the Seek → Move → Make cycle. Build friendship toward 8 (Friendship trophy). Hold 3 spell cards (Spell trophy). Complete a second quest (Quest trophy). Deliver 4 apples or gems in one turn if the opportunity arises.

**The Home Stretch**: With 3 trophies, one more wins it. Reveal your personality card if it makes a trophy easier to claim. When you place your 4th trophy, the Grand Finale triggers — finish the round, then most trophies wins.
